english: a low, humming noise, rná-bai bú-ga bkág-pai tse (rna ba'i bu ga bkag pa'i tshe) _ur-_úr zés-pai sgra ('ur 'ur zhes pa'i sgra) the humming in the ears produced by stopping them Wdn., _ur-_úr-po-yi sgra ('ur 'ur po yi sgra) id. Wdn.; rná-ba _úr-la krog (rna ba 'ur la khrog) there is a buzzing in my ear S.g.; _ur ldañ ('ur ldan) or byuñ (byung) a noise is heard; Cs. more particularly: talk, babbling, chit-chat, _ur-ytón-ba ('ur gton ba) to talk, to chat; *toñ-_ur* (*tong 'ur*) C. (lit. stoñ) (stong) bragging, humbug; _ur-sgra ('ur sgra) = _ur ('ur) noise caused by many voices, many footsteps, cf. _u-sgra ('u sgra); of the howling of a tempest, _úr-sgra čé ('ur sgra che) although it (the thunder) makes a great noise Mil.; _ur-tiñ ('ur ting) a brass basin, used to make a noise by striking it Sch.; _úr-ba ('ur ba) sbst. a humming insect, beetle Sch.; vb., to be noisy, chattering, Cs.; dga-grágs _úr-te (dga' grags 'ur te) shouting, rejoicing Mil.; *_ur čò-če* (*'ur co ce*) to set a dog on a person W.; *_ur bsád-pa* (*'ur bsad pa*), _ur-brdáb btáñ-ba ('ur brdab btang ba) C. W. to exaggerate, brag, boast. — 2. bag-dró _ur-_úr (bag dro 'ur 'ur) Pth. seems to describe the feeling of a genial warmth pervading the body. — 3. *wur gyág-pa* (*wur rgyag pa*) C, *wur gyáb-če* (*wur rgyab ce*), *táñ-če* (*tang ce*) W. to smooth, v. dbur-ba (dbur ba). — 4. _úr-rdo ('ur rdo) a sling Sch., _úr-rdo _pén-pa ('ur rdo 'phen pa) to throw with a sling.

I. num. figure: 143. — II. sbst. 1. provinc. _u ('u) kiss, _o byéd-pa ('o byed pa) to kiss Lt., kă-la (kha la) on the mouth Pth.; pyag (phyag), zabs (zhabs) resp. on the hand, the foot Cs.; _o ytóñ-ba ('o gton ba) Cs., *_u lán-če* (*'u lan ce*) W, = _o byed-pa ('o byed pa). — 2. v. _o-ma ('o ma). — III. pron. 1. pers. pron. we, v. _u-čag ('u cag). — 2. dem. pron. this Cs. v. _u ('u) III. — IV. interj. (_o _ó) ('o 'o) 1. like oh, yes! as a reply: _o lágs-so ('o lags so) oh very well! Mil; *_o yóñ-ñog* (*'o yong ngog*), *_o díg-gog* (*'o dig gog*), *_o gyál-log* (*'o rgyal log*) W., *_o yóñ-ñe* (*'o yong nge*) C. well! it's all right to me! well, do so! — _o _o ('o 'o), _o _ó ('o 'o), so! well! very well! in W. it is a common reply, indicating nothing more, than that attention has been paid to the words spoken, like the English well! indeed! — 2. as a positive affirmative, yes! W., cf. _o-ná ('o na).

also with rnams (rnams) and čag (cag), (Cs. also _ú-skol ('u skol)), Ld. á-yo (a yo), we, Mil., Tar., Thgy., e.g. (if all men must die), _o-skol lta čí smos ('o skol lta ci smos) of course also we Thgy.; it is very often used as a reciprocal pronoun: _ó-skol ma ši prád-pa ('o skol ma shi phrad pa) the fact, that we have seen each other once more before we die Mil.

resp. fatigue, weariness, want, any kind of hardship, *péblam-la ob-gyúl ma kyód-da* (*pheb lam la ob gyul ma khyod da*) W. has not your walk hither fatigued you? _o-brgyál yóñ-lugs ('o brgyal yong lugs) the getting into difficulties Mil.; more frq. as vb.: _o-brgyál-ba ('o brgyal ba), kyéd-čag-rnams _o ma brgyál-lam (khyed cag rnams 'o ma brgyal lam) are you perhaps fatigued? Glr.; zabs-tóg _o mi brgyal-ba bul (zhabs tog 'o mi brgyal ba 'bul) a short expression for: everything shall be at your service, so that you shall not want anything Mil.; _o-re-brgyál ('o re brgyal) = _o-brgyál ('o brgyal) 1. trouble, drudgery, annoyance Mil. 2. decay, decline, ruin, of religion, usages etc.

lamentation, wailing, cry for help, gen. as vb. _o-dód bód-pa ('o dod 'bod pa) to lament, to call for help Glr., Pth., Wdn.; _o-dód-pa ('o dod pa) one that seeks help, support, redress, a client, a plaintiff, more in pop. language.

(cf. _o ('o), _oñ ('ong), _oñ-kyañ ('ong kyang)), comes nearest to the Greek ἀλλά, used esp. to introduce a new thought or proposition in speech: now, what shall you do in that case? Dzl.; well, what did he say? Dzl.; well, I hope you have at least... Dzl.; why, ay, Mil.; but now Thgy.; but, the Latin autem, when a new clause is added Mil., Thgy.; yea, in a climax, e.g.: I met with a naked man, yea, an insane ascetic Mil. — 2. as an answer in the affirmative, yes W. —

milk, _ó-ma jó-ba ('o ma 'jo ba) to milk Glr.; snyól-ba (snyol ba) to let it curdle Cs., srúb-pa (srub pa) to churn it Cs.; _ó-ma čags ('o ma chags) the milk thickens, coagulates Cs. — Comp. _o-táñ ('o thang) 'milk-meadow', the plain in which Lhasa now stands; of the former lake, _o-táñ-gi mtso ('o thang gi mtsho) Glr., a sedgy moor is said to be still remaining. — _o-túg ('o thug) milk-soup Tar. — _o-túd ('o thud) cheese, v. tud (thud). — _o-túñ ('o 'thung) suckling-child, baby, = žo-túñ (zho 'thung). — _o-
